franchise.name: Final Fantasy
franchise.description: Final Fantasy is a science fiction and fantasy anthology media franchise created by Hironobu Sakaguchi and developed by Square (now Square Enix). The franchise centers on a series of role-playing video games, but has expanded into films, anime, manga, novels, audio dramas, and other media. Each main installment is typically set in a different universe with distinct characters and stories, though recurring elements include chocobos, moogles, magic, crystals, and characters named Cid.

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy
videoGames.notes: Originally released for NES; multiple remasters and ports

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy II
videoGames.notes: Introduced character leveling system based on actions

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy III
videoGames.notes: Introduced the job system

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy IV
videoGames.notes: First game with active time battle system

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy V
videoGames.notes: Refined job system

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy VI
videoGames.notes: First game directed by Yoshinori Kitase

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy VII
videoGames.notes: First 3D entry; franchise breakthrough in the West

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy VIII
videoGames.notes: Introduced junction system; realistic character proportions

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy IX
videoGames.notes: Return to series roots with fantasy setting

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy X
videoGames.notes: First game with voice acting; introduced sphere grid

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy X-2
videoGames.notes: First direct sequel in main series; job-based battle system

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XI
videoGames.notes: First MMORPG in the series; servers still active

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XII
videoGames.notes: Introduced gambit system; The Zodiac Age remaster

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XIII
videoGames.notes: First multiplatform mainline entry

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XIII-2
videoGames.notes: Direct sequel with monster taming mechanics

videoGames.title: Lightning Returns: Final Fantasy XIII
videoGames.notes: Action-focused combat; time management system

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XIV
videoGames.notes: Original version discontinued 2012; relaunched as A Realm Reborn (2013)

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XV
videoGames.notes: Open world; originally developed as Final Fantasy Versus XIII

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy XVI
videoGames.notes: Action-focused combat; rated M for mature

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
videoGames.notes: First part of the Final Fantasy VII remake project

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
videoGames.notes: Second part of the Final Fantasy VII remake project

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy Tactics
videoGames.notes: Tactical RPG set in Ivalice; directed by Yasumi Matsuno

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles
videoGames.notes: Action RPG with cooperative multiplayer focus

videoGames.title: Final Fantasy Type-0
videoGames.notes: Originally Final Fantasy Agito XIII; dark war story

videoGames.title: Dissidia Final Fantasy
videoGames.notes: Crossover fighting game featuring series characters

videoGames.title: Stranger of Paradise: Final Fantasy Origin
videoGames.notes: Action game set in Final Fantasy I universe; Team Ninja collaboration

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy Brave Exvius
mobileGames.notes: Pixel art RPG with original characters and series cameos

mobileGames.title: War of the Visions: Final Fantasy Brave Exvius
mobileGames.notes: Tactical RPG set in the Brave Exvius universe

mobileGames.title: Mobius Final Fantasy
mobileGames.notes: Original story with job system; servers closed 2021

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy Record Keeper
mobileGames.notes: Pixel art homage to series; global version closed 2022

mobileGames.title: Dissidia Final Fantasy Opera Omnia
mobileGames.notes: Turn-based RPG with Dissidia characters; global service ended 2024

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy Airborne Brigade
mobileGames.notes: Social card battle game; one of the earliest FF mobile titles

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy All the Bravest
mobileGames.notes: Simple touch-based RPG; removed from stores

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy Dimensions
mobileGames.notes: Original 2D RPG with job system; episodic format

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy Dimensions II
mobileGames.notes: Sequel to Dimensions; time travel story

mobileGames.title: Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster Series
mobileGames.notes: Remastered versions of Final Fantasy I through VI

mobileGames.title: Chocobo GP
mobileGames.notes: Kart racing game featuring Chocobo and Final Fantasy characters

films.title: Final Fantasy: The Spirits Within
films.notes: First photorealistic CGI film; directed by series creator

films.title: Final Fantasy VII: Advent Children
films.notes: Sequel to Final Fantasy VII set two years later

films.title: Final Fantasy VII: Advent Children Complete

films.title: Kingsglaive: Final Fantasy XV
films.notes: Prequel film to Final Fantasy XV featuring Nyx Ulric

tvSeries.title: Final Fantasy: Legend of the Crystals
tvSeries.notes: Anime sequel set 200 years after Final Fantasy V

tvSeries.title: Final Fantasy: Unlimited
tvSeries.notes: Original anime series with unique setting and characters

tvSeries.title: Final Fantasy XIV: Dad of Light
tvSeries.notes: Japanese drama about father and son bonding through FFXIV

tvSeries.title: Final Fantasy IX Animated Series
tvSeries.notes: Announced 2021 by Cyber Group Studios; status currently unclear

books.title: Final Fantasy XV: The Dawn of the Future
books.author: Jun Eishima

books.title: Final Fantasy XIV: Chronicles of Light

books.title: Final Fantasy Ultimania Series

books.notes: Comprehensive art and lore books for each entry

books.title: Final Fantasy XV: Official Works
books.author: Square Enix
books.notes: Art and development history of Final Fantasy XV

audioDramas.title: Final Fantasy X: Will
audioDramas.notes: Sequel to Final Fantasy X-2; included with HD Remaster

audioDramas.title: Final Fantasy XV: Prologue Parting Ways

webSeries.title: Final Fantasy XV: Brotherhood
webSeries.notes: Anime series exploring backstories of Noctis and his companions

webSeries.title: Final Fantasy VII Remake: Inside Final Fantasy VII Remake
webSeries.notes: Behind-the-scenes documentary on FFVII Remake development

boardGames.title: Final Fantasy: The Card Game
boardGames.notes: Collectible card game featuring characters from across the series

boardGames.title: Final Fantasy VII: Monopoly
boardGames.notes: Monopoly edition themed around Final Fantasy VII

canceledOrDefunct.notes: Planned DLC episode featuring Aranea Highwind

canceledOrDefunct.title: Final Fantasy XV: Episode Lunafreya
canceledOrDefunct.notes: Planned DLC episode focusing on Lunafreya

canceledOrDefunct.title: Final Fantasy XV: Episode Noctis
canceledOrDefunct.notes: Alternate ending DLC for Final Fantasy XV

canceledOrDefunct.title: Final Fantasy VII Remake (PS3 Tech Demo)
canceledOrDefunct.notes: 2005 tech demo that inspired the eventual full remake
